channel.title: Polymyth Seminar Calendar — Seminar Schools
channel.description: Lectures, conferences, workshops, readings, festivals, exhibitions, protests across Toronto, the GTA, Hamilton, Guelph, and Waterloo. Upcoming events from the consolidated polymyth seminar calendar.

item.title: NXNE Music Festival 2026 (31st edition)
item.description: 30+ venues across downtown Toronto — festival-of-form — 300+ artists across 30+ venues. Free FIFA viewing at Nathan Phillips Square.

item.title: Cat Clyde, Miesha and the Spanks, La Sécurité; Serena Ryder honoured: Canadian Sync Awards / NXNE 2026
item.description: The Concert Hall, 888 Yonge St, Toronto — panel — Canadian Sync Awards within NXNE. Serena Ryder receives the GMSC Impact Award.

item.title: Pete Crighton: The Vinyl Diaries
item.description: Jack Rabinovitch Reading Room, Toronto Reference Library, 789 Yonge Street — reading

item.title: Contact Matthew Leisinger, mleising@yorku.ca: YUSEMP Third Annual Meeting
item.description: York University — workshop — York University Seminar in Early Modern Philosophy.

item.title: Andrew Franklin-Hall, Eric Mathison (organizers): Third Annual Toronto Bioethics Workshop
item.description: Jackman Humanities Building, JHB 100, 170 Saint George Street — panel

item.title: Mississauga Halal Food Festival 2026
item.description: Celebration Square, Mississauga — cultural-reproduction

item.title: TBA; contact Matthew Leisinger, mleising@yorku.ca: YUSEMP Third Annual Meeting (York University Seminar in Early Modern Philosophy)
item.description: York University — lecture

item.title: Caribana Official Launch 2026
item.description: Scarborough Town Centre, Toronto — cultural-reproduction — Toronto Caribbean Carnival official 2026 kickoff with live performances. 2-8 PM.

item.title: The Algorithmic State: Sovereignty, Safety, and the Governance of Public Sector AI
item.description: Online (Munk School) — lecture

item.title: Terry Tempest Williams: The Glorians
item.description: Bram and Bluma Appel Salon, Toronto Reference Library, 789 Yonge Street — reading

item.title: Ideas & Ideals: The Ethics of Civic Responsibility
item.description: Massey College, 4 Devonshire Place, Toronto — lecture — Massey College Ethics Series. What does it mean for a synagogue or a community institution to be a good citizen?

item.title: Toronto Jazz Festival 2026 (39th edition)
item.description: Bloor-Yorkville (free) + Koerner Hall, Jazz Bistro, Hugh's Room Live, Danforth Music Hall, Phoenix — festival-of-form — 10-day 39th-edition festival. Cassandra Wilson 30th-anniversary New Moon Daughter; Hiromi's Sonicwonder; Ibrahim Maalouf T.O.M.A.; Norma Winstone; Laila Biali; Emilie-Claire Barlow.

item.title: Red Revue Concert — Summer Solstice Celebration
item.description: Aga Khan Museum — performance

item.title: Laila Biali at Jazz Bistro (Toronto Jazz Festival)
item.description: Jazz Bistro, 251 Victoria St, Toronto — festival-of-form — JUNO-winning vocalist Laila Biali with two-night stand at Jazz Bistro.

item.title: Julia Hotz: The Connection Cure
item.description: Bram and Bluma Appel Salon, Toronto Reference Library, 789 Yonge Street — reading

item.title: Emilie-Claire Barlow, François Richard: Emilie-Claire Barlow at Koerner Hall (Toronto Jazz Festival)
item.description: Koerner Hall, Royal Conservatory of Music, 273 Bloor St W, Toronto — festival-of-form — La plus belle saison with pianist François Richard.

item.title: AGO Annual General Meeting
item.description: Art Gallery of Ontario — gathering

item.title: Véronique Munoz-Dardé (UCL/Berkeley, second-session commentator); other invited participants: Stephen J. White (Northwestern), Rahul Kumar (Queen's); co-organized by Louis-Philippe Hodgson (York) and Joseph Heath (Toronto); manuscript at http://www.ajjulius.net/reconstruction.pdf; RSVP lhodgson@yorku.ca: Workshop on A.J. Julius's manuscript "Reconstruction" — three sessions of comments plus author reply plus general discussion
item.description: Centre for Ethics, U of T — workshop

item.title: TBA: Beyond Earth: A Conversation on Artemis, Leadership, and Public Engagement
item.description: Aga Khan Museum — lecture

item.title: Hito Steyerl S.D. Clark Lecture
item.description: JHI — lecture

item.title: Eve Egoyan (JHI Artist in Residence): Eve Egoyan *In Stone* premiere
item.description: JHI — lecture

item.title: Pride Toronto 2026 (45th anniversary, 'We Won't Stop')
item.description: Church-Wellesley Village + Yonge Street parade route, Toronto — cultural-reproduction — 45th-anniversary edition. 8 stages, 300+ performers. Theme: We Won't Stop.

item.title: Toronto Trans March (Pride Toronto 2026)
item.description: Church-Wellesley Village, Toronto — gathering

item.title: Norma Winstone, Mike Murley: Norma Winstone with Atlantic Jazz Collective (Toronto Jazz Festival)
item.description: Jazz Bistro, 251 Victoria St, Toronto — festival-of-form

item.title: Mississauga Taco Fest 2026 (debut)
item.description: Port Credit Memorial Park, Mississauga — cultural-reproduction

item.title: Toronto Dyke March (Pride Toronto 2026)
item.description: Church-Wellesley Village, Toronto — gathering

item.description: Koerner Hall, 273 Bloor St W, Toronto — festival-of-form

item.description: Danforth Music Hall, 147 Danforth Ave, Toronto — festival-of-form

item.title: Pride Toronto Parade 2026
item.description: Yonge Street (Bloor & Church to Yonge & Dundas), Toronto — gathering — Annual Pride Parade. 2:00 PM start, 45th-anniversary edition.

item.title: Rhythms of Canada
item.description: Aga Khan Museum — festival — Canada Day programming.

item.title: CFP: Toronto Workshop on Moral Psychology and Moral Theory
item.description: Department of Philosophy, University of Toronto, JHB, 170 St George St — cfp — Submission deadline. Workshop Nov 7-8, 2026. Topics: Philosophy of Cognitive Science, Value Theory.

item.title: CFP: International Association for Philosophy of Time (Eastern APA 2027)
item.description: Submission deadline (conference: Boston, Jan 13–16 2027) — cfp — Abstract deadline for the IAPT session at the 2027 Eastern APA.

item.title: CFP: University of Toronto Philosophy Graduate Conference 2026
item.description: Department of Philosophy, University of Toronto, JHB, 170 St George St — cfp — Submission deadline 11:59pm EST. Conference Oct 16-17, 2026. Keynotes Don Garrett (NYU), Alva Noe (UC Berkeley).

item.title: 2026 Summer Camps
item.description: Aga Khan Museum — gathering

item.title: The Impressionist Revolution: Monet to Matisse
item.description: Art Gallery of Ontario — exhibition — Members preview June 24; public July 7. 50 works organized by the Dallas Museum of Art; Canadian debut.

item.title: The Secret Chord: A Leonard Cohen Experience
item.description: Young Centre for the Performing Arts — performance — Soulpepper 2026/27.

item.title: TD Salsa on St. Clair 2026
item.description: St. Clair Ave W between Winona Dr & Christie St, Toronto — cultural-reproduction — Toronto Latin Street Festival. Sat 11am-9:30pm, Sun 11am-8:30pm.

item.title: TD Festival of South Asia 2026 (24th edition)
item.description: Gerrard India Bazaar, Gerrard St E between Coxwell & Glenside, Toronto — cultural-reproduction

item.description: Scarborough Town Centre, Toronto — cultural-reproduction

item.title: Twelfth Night (Dream in High Park)
item.description: High Park Amphitheatre — performance — Canadian Stage Dream in High Park.

item.title: Hamilton Fringe Festival 2026
item.description: Theatre Aquarius, Mills Hardware, Players' Guild, The Westdale, The Staircase, Hamilton — festival-of-form — Hamilton Fringe Festival. Shows begin July 16.

item.title: Hillside Festival 2026
item.description: Guelph Lake Conservation Area, 7743 Conservation Rd, Guelph — festival-of-form — Three-day music festival at Guelph Lake Island. Shuttle from Guelph downtown.

item.title: BollywoodMonster Mashup 2026
item.description: Celebration Square, Mississauga — cultural-reproduction — Canada's largest Bollywood festival.

item.title: Massey College Alumni Association AGM & Summer BBQ
item.description: Massey College, 4 Devonshire Place, Toronto — gathering — Alumni Association annual general meeting followed by a summer BBQ. An afternoon and evening of friendship, conversation, and community.

item.title: Calypso Monarch competitors: OCPA Calypso Showcase (Caribana)
item.description: Toronto — performance

item.title: Toronto Caribbean Carnival 2026 (Caribana, 59th year)
item.description: Exhibition Place + Lakeshore Boulevard, Toronto — cultural-reproduction — North America's largest Caribbean carnival. 59th year. Grand Parade Aug 1.

item.description: Exhibition Place to Lakeshore Boulevard, Toronto — cultural-reproduction — Grand Parade 8 AM to 8 PM along Lakeshore.

item.title: Bill Durst, Sass Jordan, The Fabulous Thunderbirds: TD Kitchener Blues Festival 2026
item.description: City Hall main stage, King Street, Kitchener + Clock Tower at Victoria Park — festival-of-form — 35+ bands on 4 stages over 4 days. Fundraising concert Aug 6.
